summary: SWE-EVO is a strict long-horizon coding-agent benchmark where low gpt-5 resolution rates appear tied more to bounded instruction-following and benchmark demands than to missing PR or issue context alone.

bounded_claims.claim_text: SWE-EVO evaluates longer-horizon software-evolution work that is broader in scope than typical short bug-fix benchmarks.
bounded_claims.bounds: The benchmark design supports a broader-scope characterization because tasks use release-note specifications over full pre-release repositories and large multi-file gold patches, but this is a scope description rather than a causal explanation of agent failure or a definitive cross-benchmark equivalence claim.

bounded_claims.claim_text: gpt-5-08-07 resolved far fewer SWE-EVO tasks than SWE-bench Verified tasks even with release-note plus PR/issue context.
bounded_claims.bounds: The reported rates establish a large performance gap and show that added context helped only modestly on SWE-EVO, but they do not isolate a single mechanism for the gap or prove which benchmark differences are causally responsible.
